question: A mass of 100 kilograms is pushed along the floor at a constant rate of 2 m/sec. If the coefficient of sliding friction is 0.25, at what rate is work being done in watts, in horsepower?
previous_answer: 490 watts, 0.66 hp
updated_answer: The force of kinetic friction is (F_k=mu N=mu mg=0.25(100,	ext{kg})(9.8,	ext{m/s}^2)=245,	ext{N}).  The power (rate of work) is (P=F_kv=245,	ext{N}	imes2,	ext{m/s}=490,	ext{W}).
